content.text:
You are an expert cardiac electrophysiology and quantitative pharmacology assistant.

Your task is to write ONLY the LaTeX text for the Results section of a manuscript, describing simulation results for quinidine.

In THIS response, you must ONLY describe the Results subsections corresponding to Figures 9–12. Do NOT mention any figures prior to Figure 9 and beyond Figure 12. Every Figures need to be explained in one paragraph.

Structure:
- Write 4 comprehensive paragraphs, one for each of Figures 9–12, in order.

Fixed meanings:
- Figure 9: transient outward current over time, with the LaTex label {fig:time-series-ito}.
- Figure 10: hERG current over time, with the LaTex label {fig:time-series-ikr}.
- Figure 11: net inward and outward current (iNet) over time, with the LaTex label {fig:time-series-inet}.
- Figure 12: AUC of net inward and outward current (qNet) over time, with the LaTex label {fig:time-series-inet-auc}.

STRICT LATEX RULES:
- Output MUST be valid LaTeX body content only (no \documentclass, no preamble, no \begin{document}).
- Do not make any new section or subsection.
- Do NOT use Markdown at all: no backticks, no ``` fences, no # headings.
- Do NOT use any LaTeX SI-unit helper commands (such as those from the siunitx package). Write all units explicitly as plain text (e.g., ms, nA/uF, nM) next to the numbers, either in text or math mode.
- Use standard LaTeX sectioning commands such as \section, \subsection, \paragraph as needed.
- You may use math mode with $...$ or \( ... \), but do NOT wrap the entire answer in a single math environment.
- Use ASCII characters only; avoid Unicode symbols.
- Make sure all braces { } and math modes are balanced.

Please generate the LaTeX text for the Results subsections corresponding to Figures 9–12 only, using the attached CSV time-series data, features summary, and LaTeX template as guidance. Follow the style and level of detail implied by the template.
